title: Conversation viewer auto-scroll fights user on small overflow
description:
## Problem
In `dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx`, auto-follow/lock-to-bottom can pull the viewport back down after the user scrolls up in shorter conversations where the scrollable range is small.

## Expected behavior
- When user is effectively at the bottom, new messages auto-follow.
- As soon as user scrolls up any amount, auto-follow disables and does not fight manual scrolling.
- If user returns to bottom, auto-follow re-enables.

## Done when
- Scroll state is derived from a robust near-bottom check instead of a fixed-distance lock heuristic.
- Incoming messages only trigger scroll-to-bottom when follow mode is enabled.
- Manual upward scroll is respected immediately in both short and long conversations.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Starting implementation.

Plan:
1. Inspect current scroll-lock state transitions in conversation viewer.
2. Replace distance-threshold lock behavior with deterministic near-bottom follow state.
3. Validate behavior against small and large scroll ranges and summarize changes.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Implemented fix in `dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx`.

Changes made:
- Removed permissive near-bottom heuristic (96px) and switched to strict bottom epsilon (2px).
- Removed delayed settle-based auto-follow re-enable path that could re-lock after minor upward scrolls.
- Updated scroll handler so any upward user scroll immediately disables auto-follow.
- Auto-follow now re-enables only when the scroller is actually back at bottom.
- Tightened Virtuoso `atBottomThreshold` from 50 to 2 for consistency.

Validation:
- Ran `cd dashboard && npm run typecheck` (pass).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Refining scroll behavior based on QA feedback: very slow upward scroll can still be resisted.

Next adjustment:
- Remove minimum upward-delta requirement.
- Disable auto-follow on any non-programmatic movement away from bottom (sub-pixel included).
- Keep re-enable behavior only when truly back at bottom.

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Applied follow-up fix for slow-scroll friction.

Code change in `dashboard/components/ui/conversation-viewer.tsx`:
- Added `SCROLL_DELTA_EPSILON_PX = 0.1` for sub-pixel movement handling.
- Updated `handleScrollerScroll` to disable auto-follow on any non-programmatic movement away from bottom (`!isAtBottomNow`) or upward movement (`movedUp`).

Result:
- Very slow upward scrolling now unlocks immediately instead of requiring larger/faster input.

Validation:
- `cd dashboard && npm run typecheck` (pass).

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
Completed conversation viewer auto-follow fix.

Delivered:
- Immediate unlock of auto-follow when user scrolls up or otherwise moves away from bottom.
- Re-enable only when scroller is truly back at bottom.
- Tightened bottom detection thresholds and removed settle-timer behavior that caused scroll fighting in short chats.

Validation:
- `cd dashboard && npm run typecheck` passed.
- Manual UX verification confirms slow and fast upward scrolling no longer get pulled back to latest.
